topic: Why Tsunamis Can Travel Across Oceans at 500 MPH
title: How a giant wave keeps its speed across the sea

lesson.learn:
Tsunamis travel so fast because they are not normal surface waves. They are long-wavelength pulses that move through the full depth of the ocean, which lets them carry huge amounts of energy across vast distances. In deep water, the wave can race along at jet-like speeds even when the surface looks calm.

The danger appears when the wave reaches shallow water. Then the bottom slows it down, the wave compresses, and the height can grow dramatically. That is why a tsunami can cross an ocean with little warning and then become devastating near shore.
lesson.simpler: A tsunami is a very long wave that moves through the whole depth of the ocean. It can travel incredibly fast until shallow water makes it rise up.
lesson.keyTakeaway: Tsunamis are fast in deep water and dangerous in shallow water because their energy gets squeezed upward.
lesson.deeperDive:
Most tsunamis begin with sudden seafloor movement from an earthquake, landslide, volcanic eruption, or impact. That displacement pushes an enormous column of water, and the disturbance spreads outward as a long wave. Because the wavelength is so large, the energy is distributed through the depth of the sea instead of just the surface.

As the wave enters shallower water, friction with the bottom reduces speed, but the energy has to go somewhere. The result is shoaling: the wave compresses and grows taller. Warnings and evacuation timing matter because the ocean can look deceptively ordinary until the wave front arrives.
lesson.funFact: The ocean can look calm right before a tsunami reaches shore.
references: United States Geological Survey - tsunami basics

places: Ocean basins - where waves cross long distances
Warning centers - where alerts are issued
years: 2004 - Indian Ocean tsunami changes global awareness
concepts: Shoaling - wave height increasing in shallow water
Wave speed - very high in deep ocean
Wave period - the long timing between crests
Evacuation zone - the area that needs quick action
